creator: mmokhi@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Markus Kohlmeyer from comment #7)
> Is -DWITH_SSL=${OPENSSLBASE} in new line 116 correct?
In which context of meaning you mean by 'correct' ?
If you mean as it works? i should say 'yes, it's correct :D' Because QA-confirmed.

> It is right that we prefer OpenSSL as the bundled SSLlib does not support RSA which is requiered for the planned upcomming change of the default password plugin from mysql_native to sha256_password with MySQL 5.8.

Not just because of this. the main reason is the policy that we prefer FreeBSD base/packages over bundled ones as much as we can. (because of security, stability, ...)
Also i like to notice that ${OPENSSLBASE} is not OpenSSL necessarily.
It's SSL used in base (that can be LibreSSL, if you used it instead of OpenSSL. AFAIK)

Regards, Mokhi.
